response_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to use parser:writes like the yanked example and single quotes for string literals to match assertions. They provided a snippet with parser:write calls. They want to replace them with parser:writes using a table of strings, similar to the example:

parser:writes {
    'ev',
    'ent: message
',
    'da',
    'ta: {"a":1}

',
    ...
}

So for each test, they want to combine multiple write calls into a single parser:writes call with string pieces broken maybe arbitrarily? The example splits strings into pieces: they break "event: message
" into 'ev','ent: message
'? Actually example shows: 'ev','ent: message
' which concatenates to "event: message
". Similarly 'da','ta: {"a":1}

' => "data: {"a":1}

". So they want to split each string into two parts: first two characters then the rest? Not necessarily; they just want to show using parser:writes with multiple parts. In our case we can replace parser:write calls with parser:writes { ... } using the same splitting pattern.

Test 1: multiple data events across separate writes:

original writes: write1 = "event: message
data: {"x":10}

"
write2 = "event: message
data: {"y":20}

"
parser:write(write1)
parser:write(write2)

We can replace with parser:writes { 'ev','ent: message
data: {"x":10}

', 'ev','ent: message
data: {"y":20}

' } but need to preserve single quotes. Also note the original strings have escaped double quotes. In single-quoted Lua string, you can include double quotes without escaping. So we can write 'event: message
data: {"x":10}

'. That's fine.

But to match the example, maybe split each into two parts: 'ev','ent: message
data: {"x":10}

'? The example splits after 'ev' (2 chars) and before rest. Let's follow that pattern for consistency.

Test 2: repeated split write of data events:

write1 = "event: message
"
write2 = "data: {"x":10}

"
write3 = "event: message
"
write4 = "data: {"y":20}

"

We can combine into parser:writes { 'ev','ent: message
', 'da','ta: {"x":10}

', 'ev','ent: message
', 'da','ta: {"y":20}

' } but careful escaping.

Better to produce final code with parser:writes.

Thus rewrite both it blocks.

Let's produce code preserving indentation.

messages.content:
## Ground rules:
- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- DO NOT wrap answers in markdown code blocks, which means no triple backticks like ``` nor single backtick like ` .
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Recent yanks (copy to clipboard):
```lua/ask-openai/backends/sse/data_only_parser.tests.lua
                    parser:writes {
                        'ev',
                        'ent: message\n',
                        'da',
                        'ta: {"a":1}\n\n',
                        'ev',
                        'ent: message\n',
                        'da',
                        'ta: {"b":2}\n\n',
                    }
                    assert.are.same({ '{"a":1}', '{"b":2}' }, events)
```

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Lua Code Preferences

Naming
- Use snake_case for functions and variables.
- Use PascalCase for classes.

Type Hints
- Prefer EmmyLua/LuaLS style annotations
- Always annotate when type inference would be ambiguous, or for public APIs.

Examples:
```lua
---@param names string[]
---@param checker fun(name: string): boolean
---@return table<string, integer>
function process_names(names, checker) end

---@class WindowController
---@field window hs.axuielement
---@field title? string
---@field size { w: number, h: number }
local WindowController = {}
```

use parser:writes like the yanked example, and single quotes for string literals to match assertions

Here is the code I selected:
```lua/ask-openai/backends/sse/data_only_parser.tests.lua:251-269
        it("multiple data events across separate writes", function()
            local write1 = "event: message\ndata: {\"x\":10}\n\n"
            local write2 = "event: message\ndata: {\"y\":20}\n\n"
            parser:write(write1)
            parser:write(write2)
            assert.are.same({ '{"x":10}', '{"y":20}' }, events)
        end)

        it("repeated split write of data events", function()
            local write1 = "event: message\n"
            local write2 = "data: {\"x\":10}\n\n"
            local write3 = "event: message\n"
            local write4 = "data: {\"y\":20}\n\n"
            parser:write(write1)
            parser:write(write2)
            parser:write(write3)
            parser:write(write4)
            assert.are.same({ '{"x":10}', '{"y":20}' }, events)
        end)
```

This is not necessarily a complete selection of nearby code, this is just the part I want help with. Preserve indentation!
